mortgage.title: Mortgage Calculator
mortgage.subtitle: Calculate your monthly payments, total interest, and analyze early repayment strategies

method.equalInterestDesc: Fixed monthly payment, suitable for stable income
method.equalPrincipalDesc: Decreasing payment, higher initial pressure but less total interest

earlyPay.title: Early Repayment Analysis
earlyPay.tip: Tip: Repaying {amount} at month {month} can save approximately {saved} in interest

methods.title: Choosing Between Payment Methods
methods.desc: Equal payment (amortization) suits those with stable income. Equal principal suits those with higher current income or wanting to reduce total interest, with higher initial payments but about 10-20% less total interest.

earlyPay.title: Best Time for Early Repayment
earlyPay.desc: Early repayment is most effective in the first 1/3 of the loan term when interest ratio is highest. After halfway, savings are limited - funds may be better used elsewhere.

rates.title: Fixed vs Adjustable Rates
rates.desc: Fixed rates provide payment stability. Adjustable rates start lower but can increase. Choose fixed for long-term stability, adjustable if expecting rate decreases or early payoff.

compound.title: Compound Interest Calculator
compound.subtitle: See how your money grows over time with the power of compound interest

rule72.title: Rule of 72
rule72.desc: Divide 72 by your interest rate to estimate years to double your money. At 8% interest, your money doubles in approximately 9 years.

frequency.title: Compound Frequency Matters
frequency.desc: More frequent compounding means more growth. Daily compounding yields slightly more than monthly, though the difference is small at typical rates.

start.title: Start Early
start.desc: Time is your greatest ally. Starting 10 years earlier can mean 2-3x more wealth at retirement, even with smaller contributions.

currency.title: Currency Converter
currency.subtitle: Convert between 150+ currencies with real-time exchange rates
